claimText: Senna Spectabilis is an invasive plant species native to the American tropics.
evidenceExcerpt: Invasive species, Native to American tropicals.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin of invasive species.

claimText: India's first science-based, community-driven eradication of Senna Spectabilis was carried out in Wayanad, Kerala.
evidenceExcerpt: Kerala carried out India’s first science-based, community-driven eradication of Senna Spectabilis in Wayanad.
examUse: Fact-based statement on conservation efforts and locations.
distractorHooks: Eradication carried out in Tamil Nadu
First eradication done in the Himalayas

claimText: Senna Spectabilis forms dense, sterile thickets that choke out native plants, alter soil chemistry, and deprive herbivores of food.
evidenceExcerpt: Forms dense, sterile thickets, choking out native plants, altering soil chemistry and depriving herbivores of food
examUse: Statement-based question on the ecological consequences of the invasive species.
distractorHooks: Improves soil nitrogen content for native flora
Serves as a primary food source for local herbivores

claimText: Senna Spectabilis visually resembles Kanikkonna, the state flower of Kerala.
examUse: Statement-based question on plant identification and state symbols.
distractorHooks: Resembles the state flower of Karnataka
Kanikkonna is an invasive species from South America
